content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a program to help users plan their weekend activities. 

The program will gather details about their favorite activity, place to visit, number of friends, and hours spent. 

It will practice handling multiple inputs, using `.split()` for efficient input handling, `map()` for numeric conversions, and `.upper()` for formatting text in uppercase.
constraints: Each activity and place is a non‑empty string of length ≤ 100 containing no spaces
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 100). Each test case consists of two lines. The first line of a test case contains two strings without spaces: the favorite activity and the favorite place, separated by a space. The second line contains two integers: the number of friends and the number of hours, separated by a space.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two lines. The first line: "You plan to go <ACTIVITY_IN_UPPERCASE> at the <PLACE_IN_UPPERCASE>." The second line: "You will be joined by <NUM_FRIENDS> friends and will spend <HOURS> hours there."

sampleTestCases.output:
You plan to go CYCLING at the PARK.
You will be joined by 2 friends and will spend 4 hours there.
You plan to go RUNNING at the TRACK.
You will be joined by 4 friends and will spend 2 hours there.
sampleTestCases.explanation: T=2. First test case converts "Cycling" and "Park" to uppercase and prints the numbers 2 and 4. Second test case does the same for "Running" and "Track" with numbers 4 and 2.

sampleTestCases.output: You plan to go SWIMMING at the POOL.\nYou will be joined by 0 friends and will spend 0 hours there.
sampleTestCases.explanation: Single test case with zero friends and zero hours; strings are uppercased and numbers printed as‑is.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times reading the two lines of each test case. Split the first line to obtain the activity and place strings, convert both to uppercase using the language's built‑in functions. Parse the two integers from the second line. Finally print the two required sentences for each test case. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space besides the input strings.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored irrespective of T.

code.Python3:
import sys
def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().splitlines()
    if not data: return
    idx=0
    T=int(data[idx].strip()); idx+=1
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        if idx>=len(data): break
        activity,place=data[idx].split(); idx+=1
        friends,hours=map(int,data[idx].split()); idx+=1
        out.append(f"You plan to go {activity.upper()} at the {place.upper()}.")
        out.append(f"You will be joined by {friends} friends and will spend {hours} hours there.")
    sys.stdout.write("
".join(out))
if __name__=="__main__":
    main()
code.JavaScript: const fs=require('fs');\\nconst input=fs.readFileSync(0,'utf-8').trim().split('\\\\n');\\nlet idx=0;\\nif(idx>=input.length){process.exit(0);}\\nconst T=parseInt(input[idx].trim()); idx++;\\nlet out=[];\\nfor(let t=0;t<T;t++){\\n  if(idx>=input.length) break;\\n  const [activityRaw,placeRaw]=input[idx].trim().split(/\\\\s+/); idx++;\\n  const [friendsStr,hoursStr]=input[idx].trim().split(/\\\\s+/); idx++;\\n  const activity=activityRaw.toUpperCase();\\n  const place=placeRaw.toUpperCase();\\n  const friends=Number(friendsStr);\\n  const hours=Number(hoursStr);\\n  out.push(`You plan to go ${activity} at the ${place}.`);\\n  out.push(`You will be joined by ${friends} friends and will spend ${hours} hours there.`);\\n}\\nconsole.log(out.join('\\\\n'));
